title: Protein Per Calorie: 80+ Foods Ranked | 28grams.vip
description: Which foods give you the most protein for the fewest calories? 90 common foods ranked by protein-to-calorie ratio to help you hit your macros efficiently.

@graph.headline: Protein Per Calorie: 80+ Foods Ranked
@graph.description: Which foods give you the most protein for the fewest calories? 90 common foods ranked by protein-to-calorie ratio.

mainEntity.name: What food has the highest protein per calorie?

acceptedAnswer.text: Pure protein supplements like Whey Protein Isolate top the list with around 0.24g of protein per calorie. For whole foods, lean seafood like Shrimp and Tuna, and egg whites are exceptionally high, offering over 0.20g of protein per calorie.

mainEntity.name: Why is the protein to calorie ratio important?

acceptedAnswer.text: When trying to build muscle while losing fat (body recomposition) or simply maintaining a calorie deficit, you need to hit your daily protein targets without exceeding your calorie limit. A high protein-to-calorie ratio makes this much easier.

mainEntity.name: Are plant-based proteins efficient per calorie?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most plant-based sources like beans and lentils are less efficient per calorie compared to lean meats because they naturally contain carbohydrates. However, isolated plant proteins like Seitan (vital wheat gluten) are extremely efficient, rivaling lean meats.

mainEntity.name: How is the protein per calorie ratio calculated?

acceptedAnswer.text: It is calculated by dividing the total grams of protein by the total calories in a given serving. For example, if 100g of chicken breast has 22.5g of protein and 106 calories, the ratio is 22.5 / 106 = ~0.21g per calorie.

mainEntity.name: Is a higher ratio always better?

acceptedAnswer.text: Not necessarily. While a high ratio is great for pure protein intake, your body also needs healthy fats (from foods like salmon, nuts, and avocados) and complex carbohydrates for overall health and energy.
